chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail Application No.S-340 of 2019 _______________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For order on MA 3042/2019. 2. For order on MA 3043/2019. 3. For hearing of bail application 18-06-2019 Mr. Bakhshan Khan Mahar, Advocate for applicants along with applicants. 1. Granted. 2. Granted. 3. Applicants Allah Wasayo, Saifullah, Khadim, Ghulam Sarwar, Ghulam Rasool alias Aftab Ahmed, Sikander, Hadi Bux, Rasool Bux @ Nandho, Sodoro, Abdul Haq and Muhammad Sulleman all by caste Mhar, have approached this Court for grant of protective bail in Crime No.27/2019, u/s 324, 452, 114, 427, 147, 148, 149, 337-H PPC, registered at Police Station Chak, District Shikarpur. Learned counsel for the applicants states that the applicants are innocent and have falsely been involved by the complainant; that no independent person has been shown as a witness except the kith and kin of the complainant; that a large number of accused i.e 32 who are closely related to each other has been involved in this case to see that no male member remains out of jail; that there is a delay of 10 days in lodging of the FIR which has not been properly explained; that the applicants are ready and willing to surrender before the learned Sessions Court for grant of pre-arrest bail but they have grave apprehension that if they approach the learned Sessions Court, they will be arrested in the aforesaid Crime, therefore they have directly approached this Court. Be that as it may, the applicants are granted protective bail for a period of 10 days from today to surrender before the learned Sessions Court, upon furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/-(rupees thirty thousands) each and P.R bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court.

Learned counsel for the applicants states that the applicants are innocent and have falsely been involved by the complainant; that no independent person has been shown as a witness except the kith and kin of the complainant; that a large number of accused i.e 32 who are closely related to each other has been involved in this case to see that no male member remains out of jail; that there is a delay of 10 days in lodging of the FIR which has not been properly explained; that the applicants are ready and willing to surrender before the learned Sessions Court for grant of pre-arrest bail but they have grave apprehension that if they approach the learned Sessions Court, they will be arrested in the aforesaid Crime, therefore they have directly approached this Court. Be that as it may, the applicants are granted protective bail for a period of 10 days from today to surrender before the learned Sessions Court, upon furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/-(rupees thirty thousands) each and P.R bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of aforesaid period or whenever applicants appear before the concerned court whichever is earlier. Instant bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE
